mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income growth, which drives positive equity returns through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while simultaneously the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, this signals weakening consumer demand and labor market deterioration. The joint deterioration triggers negative equity returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS declines and UNRATE > 5.0%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.5% month-over-month indicates accelerating supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum. This signals improving corporate profitability and capital efficiency, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO > 0.5% MoM growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,800 thousand units while simultaneously real GDP growth (GDPC1) shows deceleration below 2.0% annualized. This joint signal indicates weakening demand-side momentum and construction sector contraction, predicting negative 60-day equity returns as forward growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1800 thousand AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both HOUST < 1800 and GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth falls below 2.5% while the federal funds rate remains above 3.5%, the real interest rate becomes restrictively high. This regime signals monetary policy is tightening in real terms, compressing equity valuations and producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or the real rate interaction term has p-value > 0.15 in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines below 4.5% for two consecutive months, signaling tight labor market conditions and wage pressure. This triggers positive equity returns over 5 trading days as investors interpret tight labor markets as evidence of strong demand and pricing power, offsetting inflation concerns.
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong demand
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following UNRATE < 4.5% for 2+ months are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while simultaneously nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) shows month-over-month growth below 50,000 persons. This divergence signals supply-side weakness coupled with labor market softening, predicting negative 60-day equity returns as forward earnings are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND PAYEMS MoM growth < 50,000
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or early contraction phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both INDPRO declines and PAYEMS < 50,000 MoM, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).
